{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.0,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Logical consistency is a central responsibility as the delegation seeks an opinion on whether the argument from metrics to conclusions holds.",
    "R1.3": "Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing because validating if the reported ROC-AUC values support the improvement claims is essential to any feedback on this results section.",
    "R1.4": "Novelty assessment is central to determining if the gains over baselines represent a meaningful contribution.",
    "R1.6": "Writing polish is central as the delegation implies evaluating the clarity and conciseness of the draft's presentation.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainty disclosure is central to ensure feedback addresses whether variabilities in results are adequately acknowledged.",
    "RX.2": "Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing to check if the described improvements are presented without exaggeration.",
    "RX.3": "Scope adherence is central to verify that the claims stay within the bounds of the DUD-E benchmark evaluation.",
    "RX.4": "Downstream-harm avoidance is peripheral but included to consider any potential risks in applying the virtual screening pipeline.",
    "RX.5": "Provenance and traceability is central to assess the reliability of the DUD-E benchmark and baseline references used."
  }
}

rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is a central responsibility as the delegation seeks an opinion on whether the argument from metrics to conclusions holds.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing because validating if the reported ROC-AUC values support the improvement claims is essential to any feedback on this results section.
rationale.R1.4: Novelty assessment is central to determining if the gains over baselines represent a meaningful contribution.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is central as the delegation implies evaluating the clarity and conciseness of the draft's presentation.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is central to ensure feedback addresses whether variabilities in results are adequately acknowledged.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is load-bearing to check if the described improvements are presented without exaggeration.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is central to verify that the claims stay within the bounds of the DUD-E benchmark evaluation.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is peripheral but included to consider any potential risks in applying the virtual screening pipeline.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability is central to assess the reliability of the DUD-E benchmark and baseline references used.
